agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Sprint 33 / Session 195 -- US-329 BLOCKED (filed offices/pm/blockers/BL-016.md; emitted HUMAN_INTERVENTION_REQUIRED). US-329 ('drive_counter compute-from-drive_summary', S/P3, last passes:false story in V0.27.7) cannot be executed as written. Pre-flight (`rg drive_counter src/pi/sync/ src/server/api/ src/server/services/` + repo-wide `[Dd]rive[_]?[Cc]ounter`): (1) SCOPE PREMISE FALSE -- there is NO `SELECT FROM drive_counter` anywhere; the server-side table is write-only -- only `runDriveCounterUpsert` (sync.py) writes it + the `DriveCounterData` Pydantic field; `analysis.py` has a comment mention only; `v0001_*` migration has the DDL; NO dashboard route / report.py / health endpoint reads `last_drive_id`. So acceptance #2/#3 ('computed from MAX(drive_id) IN drive_summary' / 'all read sites switched') have no read site -- this is stopCondition #1 fired in its INVERSE (used in NO way, not a cache-benefiting way). (2) INTENT IMPOSSIBLE WITHIN SCOPE -- 'drop drive_counter sync entirely' but the real path is US-314: Pi `src/pi/sync/client.py:pushDriveCounter()` POSTs `{driveCounter:{lastDriveId:N}}` -> server `runDriveCounterUpsert()` monotonic upsert; dropping it needs BOTH ends, but `src/pi/sync/client.py` is in the story's doNotTouch AND not in filesToTouch, and `SyncRequest` is `model_config=ConfigDict(extra='forbid')` (sync.py:192) so removing `DriveCounterData` server-side -> 422s every Pi sync that includes the snapshot (regression). The server-file-only scope only permits adding a computed helper nothing calls (Option C; fixes nothing). (3) OPEN PM/CIO RETARGET CONFLICT -- tester's 2026-05-12 db-review note (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-tester-db-review-validation-bug-vs-techdebt.md, 'CONFLICT TO RESOLVE' + ACTIONS #1/#3): US-329's 'keep table + compute' framing vs CIO's 'DROP TABLE drive_counter -- serves no purpose'; tester recommends retarget US-329 -> 'stop maintaining server-side drive_counter; consumers compute from drives; Pi stops POSTing' with the actual DROP TABLE riding a new V0.28 'server schema normalization' epic; explicitly returns it to Marcus+CIO sign-off. Prior Session 194 close note also flagged 'both are PM/CIO calls before the next iteration picks up US-329'. (Aside also unresolved by the story: server-side `drive_summary.drive_id` is NULL for every Pi-synced row -- the drive number lives in `source_id`; US-326 [shipped this sprint] heals `drive_id` going forward but doesn't backfill -- so 'compute from drive_summary' must also pick MAX(source_id) vs MAX(drive_id) vs MAX(COALESCE(drive_id,source_id)).) BL-016 lays out Option A (retarget per tester; re-spec to add `src/pi/sync/client.py` to scope) / Option B (defer US-329 to the V0.28 epic -- RECOMMENDED; lowest-risk; the epic owns the table drop anyway; V0.27.7 then ships 4/4 -> COMPLETE) / Option C (minimal in-scope helper, NOT recommended). NO CODE WRITTEN this session (Refusal Rule 1 -- ambiguity is a blocker; Rule 3 -- scope fence). sprint.json US-329 `completionNotes` updated with the same summary; sprint_lint -> 0 errors (the US-329 `feedback:[]` warn is pre-existing for any passes:false story). Sprint 33 status: 4/5 passes:true (US-326/327/328/330); US-329 is the lone holdout, now BLOCKED pending the PM/CIO decision. NEXT ITERATION: do NOT re-pick US-329 until BL-016 is resolved + the story re-spec'd (Option A) or removed (Option B); if removed, the sprint is COMPLETE. --- PRIOR (Session 194): US-330 SHIPPED passes:true (I-030: V0.27.6 post-Drain-17 boot wrote a startup_log row with EMPTY prior_boot_clean AND prior_last_entry_ts -- regression cliff at the V0.27.6 deploy boundary). Fix = the 'race-guard' path from the US-330 scope (chosen over the out-of-scope unit-reordering path). PRE-FLIGHT (code archaeology; live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` is a CIO follow-up, same pattern as US-326 journalctl / US-327 ssh / US-328): writeStartupLog writes NULL for BOTH columns only when report.priorBootClean is None AND report.priorLastEntryTs is None == detectBootReason's `priorEntry is None` branch == `journalctl --list-boots` returned None (the runJournalctl failure sentinel: subprocess TimeoutExpired/FileNotFoundError/OSError; journalctl is on the service PATH so likely TimeoutExpired -- the 10s cap blown under boot-time I/O contention). HYPOTHESIS MAP: (C) ladder-regex-breaks RULED OUT (would give prior_boot_clean=0 with prior_last_entry_ts POPULATED -- the ts comes from --list-boots not the tail; both-NULL means the failure is upstream of graceful detection). (A) V0.27.6 US-322 orphan-cleanup.timer = LEADING -- the V0.27.5->V0.27.6 delta that fits the cliff: orphan-cleanup.timer is Persistent=true (fires at boot to catch up the missed nightly 03:00 run) and orphan-cleanup.service runs cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py --execute (a DELETE on data/obd.db on the SD card) concurrently with the launching orchestrator -> SD-card I/O starvation -> --list-boots times out -> None; the V0.27.6 deploy `enable --now`'d the timer so it fired at deploy AND next boot. (B) deploy changed boot order = not supported by code (no Before=/After= ordering moving eclipse-obd earlier; wifi.powersave drop-in could marginally hasten network.target -- a weak B-flavoured contributor at most). FIX: detectBootReason now retries `journalctl --list-boots` via the new _readBootList(journalctlRunner, sleeper) helper -- LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3 + LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_SLEEP_SECONDS=2.0 backoff, sleep DI'd via a `sleeper` kwarg threaded through recordBootReason; happy path returns on attempt 1 with zero backoff (retry cost only ever paid on the anomalous path); a non-None-but-unparseable result is parsed as-is and NOT retried (a genuine fresh Pi / pruned journal legitimately shows only the current boot); every-attempt-fails -> a WARNING ('journalctl --list-boots unavailable after N attempts ...') + the best-effort NULL row exactly as before (no silent failure -- the I-030 row was written silently; V0.24.1 anti-pattern lesson). US-308 graceful-detection body (_hasShutdownMarker / _probeLadderGraceful / SHUTDOWN_MARKERS / LADDER_GRACEFUL_* / the tail+ladder probe sequence) is BYTE-UNTOUCHED; doNotTouch honoured (no orphan-cleanup.timer change; the unit-reordering alternative IOSchedulingClass=idle / After=eclipse-obd.service on orphan-cleanup.service is filed as TD-051). FILES: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (UPDATE -- constants + _readBootList helper + sleeper kwarg on detectBootReason+recordBootReason + import time + mod-history) / tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_v0276_graceful.py (NEW -- 7 tests / 4 classes) / tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py (UPDATE -- 1-line sleeper inject). VERIFICATION all green: 7 new tests PASS; pytest tests/pi/diagnostics/ -m 'not slow' -> 98 PASS; pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' --no-cov -> exit 0; ruff clean on boot_reason.py + both test files; sprint_lint US-330 OK. CARRY for PM/CIO (see offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-rex-US-330-startup-log-fix-residual-live-confirm.md + TD-051): (1) live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` to CONFIRM the trigger; (2) Drain 18+ then a graceful boot on V0.27.7 -> startup_log row has prior_boot_clean=1; (3) acceptance #4 backfill of the empty Drain-17-boot row -- PM's call, low value, I'd skip; (4) Still open: BL-014 (harness .claude/commands write gate from US-318).

agents.name: Agent2
agents.note: Sprint 25 / Session 158 -- US-286 SHIPPED passes:true. tests/pi/integration/test_engine_drive_capture_e2e.py (1 class / 1 test / 7 phases): real ApplicationOrchestrator + DriveDetector + SummaryRecorder + ObdDataLogger; mock surface = connection.obd.query (BT edge) only; 0.16s call duration vs 30-sec budget. Mirrors US-260 cold-start pattern. Discriminator: would FAIL against May-4-5 production regression class (DriveDetector never operational) + against any silent realtime_data write regression. Two gotchas surfaced for future tests: (1) e2e fixtures must seed profiles('daily') because realtime_data has FK + bare orchestrator doesn't run ProfileManager; (2) production order query->logReading->_handleReading means first N ticks land with drive_id NULL -- query realtime_data without drive_id filter. Standing by post-US-286.
